text.title: growth-landing-page-design Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for growth-landing-page-design as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent-polished testimonials with fabricated specifics (company names, exact numbers not from the original quote).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fabricated testimonials are FTC violations and destroy trust when discovered. Agent should format and polish real quotes, never invent specifics.

failure-mode.description: Multiple CTAs on the same page section.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multiple CTAs create decision paralysis and split attention. Each section must have one primary action. Secondary CTAs belong in the footer or nav only.

failure-mode.description: Generic hero headline that could apply to any product in the category.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Generic headlines do not differentiate the product from competitors. Specificity in the ICP pain and the outcome is required to stop the scroll.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Over 50% of landing page traffic is mobile. Above-the-fold elements on mobile differ significantly from desktop. Agent audits do not catch visual layout issues on small screens.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each second of load time past 2 seconds reduces conversion rate by approximately 4.4%. This is a technical issue (images, CDN, JS bloat), not a copy issue — run Lighthouse to identify slow resources and optimize before final page launch.
